name: Montana Capital Car Title Loans
description_short: At 4411 E Belmont Ave, Fresno, CA 93702, Montana Capital Car Title Loans can be reached at +1 559-203-7116.
description_long:
MontanaCapital® has operated as a car title loan agency since 2007, reporting over 375,000 customers served across nearly two decades of operation. The company offers title-secured lending through both an online application portal and physical branch locations, with phone support at 1-888-508-5366. It holds a 4.5-star Google rating based on 59 reviews, with customers frequently citing fast processing and responsive staff.

Montana Capital provides secured loans ranging from $100 to $50,000, using the borrower's vehicle title as collateral. Borrowers retain possession of their vehicle and continue driving it throughout the loan term, receiving their title back upon final payment. Funding can occur within 24 hours of approval, with options for in-person pickup at a branch location or direct deposit. One customer review notes that funds may be disbursed as a Bank of America check rather than cash — a detail not prominently disclosed on the website.

The company explicitly targets borrowers with poor credit histories, accepting applicants with bad credit or prior bankruptcies. Pre-approval takes under five minutes and does not trigger a hard credit pull, meaning no score impact during the inquiry phase. The 24/7 online application and extended branch hours address accessibility for working borrowers who cannot visit during standard business hours. Their maximum loan amount of $50,000 places them well above typical payday or micro-lender limits.

Title loans carry significant financial risk regardless of lender. Montana Capital's website does not publicly disclose APR, interest rates, or fee structures — information that is essential for any borrowing decision. Defaulting on a title loan risks vehicle repossession, which can be financially devastating for borrowers who rely on their car for employment. The 59 Google reviews represent a very small sample relative to the claimed 375,000+ customer base. Borrowers should request full cost disclosures before signing any agreement and treat title loans as a last resort after exhausting lower-cost alternatives such as credit union PALs, personal loans, or employer advances.
pros: Same-day funding available — cash or direct deposit within 24 hours of approval
No credit check required — bad credit and prior bankruptcies explicitly accepted
Loan amounts up to $50,000, significantly higher than most emergency micro-lenders
Borrower keeps and drives their vehicle throughout the entire repayment period
Free pre-approval in under 5 minutes with no credit score impact
In business since 2007 with over 375,000 customers served
4.5-star Google rating with reviewers praising fast turnaround and friendly staff
cons: APR, interest rates, and fees are not disclosed anywhere on the public website
Vehicle repossession is the consequence of default — high stakes for borrowers dependent on their car
Funds may be issued as a Bank of America check rather than cash (noted in customer review, not disclosed upfront)
Only 59 Google reviews for a lender claiming 375,000+ customers — very thin public accountability record
Title loans are among the highest-cost borrowing products available; cost details require direct contact
best_for: Vehicle owners with bad credit or recent bankruptcy who need emergency funds quickly
Borrowers needing more than $1,000 who are locked out of personal loan products
Those who require same-day or next-day cash and have no other collateral-free options
Consumers who need to keep driving their vehicle while repaying a secured loan
diagnosis: Montana Capital is best suited for vehicle owners with damaged credit who need emergency funds quickly and have no access to lower-cost borrowing options. The main caveat is significant: interest rates and fees are not disclosed on the website, and defaulting puts the borrower's vehicle at risk of repossession — making this a high-stakes product that should only be used as a genuine last resort.
